step_by_step_thinking:
Enamel, being primarily composed of mineralized hydroxyapatite crystals with some organic matrix, has very low water content. This is essential for its hardness and protective role against dental caries.

The correct option based on this information would be A: 2-3%, which aligns closely with the minimal amount of water typically found within enamel to maintain its structural integrity.
